text: Table 149 defines the Payload format for the FLOGI and PLOGI ELSs and the LS_ACCs, and the parameter definitions apply to PLOGI, FLOGI, PLOGI LS_ACC, and FLOGI LS_ACC unless stated otherwise.

evidence_refs.quote: Table 149 defines the Payload format for the FLOGI and PLOGI ELSs and the LS_ACCs.  The defini-
tions of the parameters are applicable to PLOGI, FLOGI, PLOGI LS_ACC and FLOGI LS_ACC un-
less stated otherwise.

text: Word 0 contains the ELS_Command code.

evidence_refs.quote: ELS_Command code

LSB

LSB

MSB

LSB

evidence_refs.quote:
MSB
Class 1 and Class 6 Service Parameters
(16 bytes)
LSB

text: There are no separate Class 6 login Service Parameters; Class 1 service parameters are used instead.

evidence_refs.quote: There are no separate Class 6 login Service Parameters as Class 1 service parameters are used in-
stead.

(16 bytes)
LSB

LSB

MSB
Vendor Version Level
(16 bytes)
LSB

evidence_refs.quote:
MSB Services Availabilitya
(8 bytes) LSB

text: Word 31 contains Login Extension Data Length.

evidence_refs.quote: Login Extension Data Lengtha

evidence_refs.quote:
Clock Synchronization QoSa
(8 bytes)

text: Words 64 to n contain Login Extension Data, if any.

evidence_refs.quote: 64 to n
Login Extension Data (if any)

text: The Services Availability, Login Extension Data Length, Clock Synchronization QoS, and Login Extension Data fields are only present when the Payload Bit is set to one.

evidence_refs.quote:
MSB Services Availabilitya
(8 bytes) LSB
Login Extension Data Lengtha

Clock Synchronization QoSa

Login Extension Data (if any)

a These fields are only present when the Payload Bit (see 6.6.2.4.19) is set to one. When the Payload bit is
set to zero, these fields are not present in the Payload (i.e., the Payload is 116 bytes long).

text: When the Payload Bit is set to zero, the Services Availability, Login Extension Data Length, Clock Synchronization QoS, and Login Extension Data fields are not present, and the Payload is 116 bytes long.

evidence_refs.quote:
a These fields are only present when the Payload Bit (see 6.6.2.4.19) is set to one. When the Payload bit is
set to zero, these fields are not present in the Payload (i.e., the Payload is 116 bytes long).

text: If a Login of 256 bytes or more is required when the buffer conditions of the destination port are unknown, the first step in the stated timeout-avoidance procedure is to issue a Login request with the Payload Bit set to zero.

evidence_refs.quote: If a Login of 256 bytes or more is required when the buffer conditions of the destination port are un-
known, a timeout may be avoided with the following procedure:
1) issue a Login request with the Payload Bit (see 6.6.2.4.19) set to zero;

text: In that timeout-avoidance procedure, if the Query Buffer Conditions bit is set to one in the LS_ACC, an RPBC ELS is issued.

evidence_refs.quote: 2) if the Query Buffer Conditions bit (see 6.6.2.4.14) is set to one in the LS_ACC, issue an RPBC
ELS (see 4.2.37); and

text: In that timeout-avoidance procedure, if both the ELS Receive Data Field Size field in the RPBC LS_ACC Payload and the Buffer-to-buffer Receive Data_Field Size field in the Login LS_ACC Payload are at least 256 bytes, a Login request with the Payload Bit set to one is issued.

evidence_refs.quote: 3) if both the ELS Receive Data Field Size field in the RPBC LS_ACC Payload and the Buffer-to-
buffer Receive Data_Field Size field in the Login LS_ACC Payload are at least 256 bytes, issue
a Login request with the Payload Bit set to one.
